title: paspalum vaginatum var. littorale
intro: Paspalum vaginatum var. littorale, also known as seashore paspalum, is a type of tropical grass native to coastal regions of the Americas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and antifungal activities, which can be used to treat various infections and skin conditions. The health benefits of seashore paspalum include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it a valuable resource for treating burns and other skin injuries. In herbal preparations, seashore paspalum can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
